steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $ (x + 13)^2 $.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The expression $(x+5)^2 + 16(x+5) + 64$ is a trinomial with a compound base. Recognize whether it fits the perfect-square pattern $u^2 + 16u + 64 = (u+8)^2$ by setting $u = x+5$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $ (x + 13)^2 $: substituting $u = x+5$ gives $(u+8)^2 = (x+5+8)^2 = (x+13)^2$. ✓
  • Choice B — $ (x + 8)^2 $: this would come from using $x$ as the base instead of $x+5$. ✗
  • Choice C — $ 5(x + 8)^2 $: this adds an outside factor of 5 and uses the wrong binomial. ✗
  • Choice D — $ 8(x + 5)^2 $: this keeps the base $x+5$ but treats the expression like a multiple, not a completed square. ✗
A is correct.
